comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: m.twitch.tv
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://m.twitch.tv/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fm.twitch.tv%2F) ### Comment > When watching VODs on Twitch using the Samsung browser, ads will occasionally interrupt the video. This did not happen last year with AdGuard installed. Some time early this year, ads stared to appear again. No settings were changed manually, and I've verified that everything is enabled. > > I am using a Galaxy Tab S5e, and that was still the case back when ads were being blocked. > > Note: I don't know how to find information for most of the "Settings" part of this report. I didn't see those options anywhere on the app, and I don't know what half of them mean in the first place. I also don't have a better screenshot than the ad that happened to be playing. ### Screenshots

comment.body:
`https://ljc.coupang.com/api/v2/submit` - already blocked by `||ljc.coupang.com^$redirect=nooptext`
`adm.smartmediarep.com` - not found.
`app.link` - some requests blocked by `||app.link/_r?sdk=*&callback=branch_callback`

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

For videos like `https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_lChC6g7wY6k_RJkLhYwf-doyMVhN41KO4` > > Fake buffering of Type 2: Almost 100% > > 2. "Type 1"-like buffering via a South Korea server of AdGuard VPN (Type 2 doesn't occur here now) > > On Chrome: 90% (No fake buffering: 10%) > On Safari: 20-30% (No fake buffering: 70-80%) > > https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/203236#issuecomment-3941363251 > > > Playlist is a bit different buffering-wise, but I can't reproduce with the latest filters (it doesn't mean your issue shouldn't exist). > > The issue does exist, and of course I have the latest filters. I have checked it with two Macs, both on Chrome and on Safari. In addition, this issue is not restricted to playlists; even if you access a music directly (e.g. `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hXvaPyffOsg`) it occurs. > Also, I'm not the only one experiencing this, for example the author of this comment https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/203236#issuecomment-3940114657 also suffers from the same issue (I can reproduce it with the same URL, too). > > Here is how to reproduce it: > > 1. Connect to a Japan server of AdGuard VPN > 2. Open a browser (Chrome or Safari) in a NEW profile (Do NOT login to any Google or YouTube account) > 3. Type `https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_lChC6g7wY6k_RJkLhYwf-doyMVhN41KO4` directly into the search bar > 4. Click the first video > 5. Fake buffering appears. It also comes out after a click on another video. > > Sometimes it frequently occurs and sometimes it rarely does. comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: animo.sto.wiki
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Trackers)
 [https://animo.sto.wiki](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fanimo.sto.wiki)
### Comment
> Visit https://stowiki.net and the reported URL (in issue creation tool) tracker will be called by JS on the page with tracking information.
> 
> Easiest way to repro is:
> * Visit any page
> * See a POST to https://animo.sto.wiki/api/send in network inspector
> * See that the request has analytics/tracker information in it
> 
> Everything should then be straightforward for you from there. Suggest you consider blocking any tracker found.

### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 </summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/978un4rlwkcg0g4s4gc80cgcokgs004iba4cc6ll9s.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

### System configuration

Information | Value
--- | ---
AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS
DNS setup: | Device or router settings
DNS server: | AdGuard DNS Default server
System version: | `Windows 11`
Browser: | Chrome

Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 4 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
@Alex-302 I’ve noticed that on several news websites, the Ramadan countdown widget has been completely blocked along with the embedded ads (such as the Eminevim ad).

While I understand that the ad element should be filtered, the Ramadan countdown itself is actual site content and not purely an ads. Many news sites integrate sponsor ads into their Ramadan countdown widgets, which is not ideal, but blocking the entire widget also removes legitimate content.

I believe it would be more appropriate to block only the advertisement element while keeping the countdown timer visible.

Could this be adjusted to avoid breaking the countdown functionality? 🤔 

See:

incorrect blocked:

`https://www.milliyet.com.tr/` 
`https://www.posta.com.tr/`
`https://www.haber7.com/`

Not blocked yet:

`https://www.mynet.com/`
`https://www.turkiyegazetesi.com.tr/`
`https://www.trhaber.com/`
`https://www.haberler.com/`
`https://www.cnnturk.com/`

comment.body: @ntnguyen1234 This filter rule is not correct. Please avoid to block Ramadan counter timer, see: Ad image urls: `https://www.nefes.com.tr/images/eminevim_imsakiye_desktop_full_alan.png` `https://www.nefes.com.tr/images/eminevim_imsakiye_mobil_full_alan.png` Ad url: `.wrc-link` Ramadan counter time: `.wrc-timer` For example my filter rules: ```adb /images/eminevim*.$image,domain=nefes.com.tr nefes.com.tr##.wrc-link nefes.com.tr##.wrc-timer:style(color:black !important;) ``` It should look like this:

comment.body:
Unfortunately, the reported issue does not satisfy our [filters policy](https://adguard.com/kb/general/ad-filtering/filter-policy/) (not enough visitors).
Please add following rules to user rules:
```adblock
radioalacalle.com##.r34-banner
radioalacalle.com##.r34-overlay
radioalacalle.com##.list-triple
```

issue.title: audiophilemusic.io

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://audiophilemusic.io](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Faudiophilemusic.io) ### Comment > Firefox: Die Seite audiophilemusic.io wird nicht geladen. Wenn Sie geladen wird, dann wird die Wiedergabeliste (egal was für ein Album) zunächst nach drei Songs gestoppt (Play im Sperrbildschirm erforderlich), danach alle zwei Songs (wieder Play erforderlich). > > Edge: Die Seite audiophilemusic.io wird geladen und die Wiedergabeliste vollständig abgespielt. Nachdem sich der Bildschirm ausschaltet (fünf Minuten), sind Störgeräusche zu hören (wie bei der Schallplatte). > > Nur am Rande und nicht sehr wichtig: Die Fehlermeldung "Auto-Update funktioniert möglicherweise nicht" erscheint, obwohl beim Akku keine Einschränkung ausgewählt ist. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/r8ld9nqmfc0gkwgws4o888gc0co84kgg0xl8ztp1pc.jpg?nc=1)
Screenshot 2 ![Screenshot 2](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/pppmkc3c9c04wc884c0swgswc8ksc0o7s1uzbimfeg.jpg?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/6q2z94v1p20w8kkcgccso80kos8c48w75f82nc60zw.jpg?nc=1)
### System configuration Information | Value --- | --- AdGuard product: | AdGuard for Android v4.12.2 System version: | `Android, Xiaomi HyperOS für POCO 2.0.207.0VOQEUXM` Browser: | Firefox Mobile License type: | paid AdGuard mode: | VPN HTTPS filtering: | enabled Tracking protection: | **General settings**:
Strip URLs from tracking parameters DNS filtering: | server: `Adguard DNS`, ` adguard-dns.io`

AdGuard Mobile Ads

Privacy:

`Adguard Tracking-Schutzfilter`,
`Adguard Social-Media-Filter`,
`Adguard Cookie-Hinweise-Filter`,
`Adguard Pop-up-Filter`,
`Adguard-Filter gegen Mobile-App-Banner`,
`Adguard-Filter gegen andere Belästigungen`,
`Adguard Widgets-Filter`,
`Online Malicious URL Blocklist`,
`Adguard Deutscher Filter`,
`Adguard Extra`,
`HTTPS-Filterung` Browsing Security: | enabled Browsing Security statistics: | disabled Userscripts: | disabled Other extensions used: | `Keine Erweiterungen installiert, Sophos Intercept X (Deinstallation hat keine Auswirkung), keine andere Werbeblocker` ### Issue configuration [Import](https://reports.adguard.com/settings/import.html?product_type=and&product_version=4.12.2&system_version=Android%2C+Xiaomi+HyperOS+f%C3%BCr+POCO+2.0.207.0VOQEUXM&browser=firefox+mobile&filters=17.2.11&custom_filters=Adguard%2520URL-Tracking-Filter%252CAdguard%2520Tracking-Schutzfilter%252CAdguard%2520Social-Media-Filter%252CAdguard%2520Cookie-Hinweise-Filter%252CAdguard%2520Pop-up-Filter%252CAdguard-Filter%2520gegen%2520Mobile-App-Banner%252CAdguard-Filter%2520gegen%2520andere%2520Bel%25C3%25A4stigungen%252CAdguard%2520Widgets-Filter%252COnline%2520Malicious%2520URL%2520Blocklist%252CAdguard%2520Deutscher%2520Filter%252CAdguard%2520Extra%252CHTTPS-Filterung&dns_servers=Adguard+DNS%2C+adguard-dns.io&android.mode=VPN&android.system_root=false&dns.filters=Adguard%2520DNS-Filter%2520Version%25202.1.6.13&dns.filters_enabled=true&dns.enabled=true&https.filtering=true&browsing_security.enabled=true&browsing_security.statistics_enabled=false&stealth.enabled=true&stealth.strip_url=true&license_type=paid)

comment.body: Leider ist das Problem auf audiophilemusic.io trotz Neuinstallation von Adguard (und vorherige Datenlöschung) wie folgt: Firefox: Die Playlist wird gestoppt, nachdem sich der Sperrbildschirm eingeschaltet hat (es wird nur noch 1 Song gespielt). Edge / Opera: Die Playlist wird komplett gespielt, aber nachdem sich der Sperrbildschirm einschaltet hat gibt es Störgeräusche (wie bei der Festplatte). Es ist also der Sperrbildschirm, der Probleme macht (den Timer des Sperrbildschirms kann man auf maximal 10 Minuten stellen, leider nicht länger). Ohne Adguard spielt Firefox die komplette Playlist wieder. Freundliche Grüße

comment.body: Block Java is not enabled by default. Moved to Advanced settings, becayse Java is dead for modern browsers ~10 years (and NPAPI plugins). Just disable this option.
